hypothesis: A small auxiliary loss at `K=32` was slightly worse than the incumbent but close enough that aux tuning is not yet settled for the plain two-stage soft-codebook family. The remaining informative single-variable follow-up is to test a stronger `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` at the same `K=32` anchor and determine whether higher dead-feature pressure improves utilization enough to beat the current frontier.
summary: Run one parameter-only follow-up at `K=32` with `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.2e-3` fixed while changing only `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.01` to `0.0625`. Success criterion: beat the current `K=32` frontier anchor FVU (`0.08386963605880737`) and resolve whether aux tuning has any remaining value for the non-routed mid-K recipe.
self_review: This follows the precommitted next step from round 100 and keeps strict one-variable isolation at a frontier-relevant anchor. The main risk is that stronger aux pressure will further degrade FVU, but that would cleanly close the aux-tuning branch and justify moving on to a different scalar or family next round.
notes_to_memory: Round 100 showed `AUXK_ALPHA=0.01` at `K=32` was close but still slightly worse than the incumbent `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` anchor.
Round 101 should test the remaining stronger aux setting at the same `K=32` anchor before abandoning aux tuning on `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`.
Keep all recipe fields fixed except `AUXK_ALPHA` so the result cleanly answers whether stronger dead-feature pressure helps the plain soft-codebook mid-K lane.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0625` wins at `K=32`, propagate that aux setting to `K=24` under the same fixed recipe to test whether the gain generalizes.
If it is still close but behind at `K=32`, settle `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` as the best plain-family mid-K recipe and switch the next cleanup round to an LR sweep.
If it clearly loses at `K=32`, conclude that aux tuning is exhausted for this anchor and move the next round to a different single-variable dimension such as learning rate or a new family.
